failures: spec_under_150_lines (154 lines — 4 over, tradeoff from prose-based approach)
no_mechanism_tables (shortcut→store method mapping in Data & IO)

delta.time_change: +14% (prose-based approach takes slightly longer to generate)
qualitative_improvements: No more Prisma schema syntax or SQL in specs
New files described as roles not exact paths
Entry Points replaced with Key Modules (less prescriptive)

analyst_observations: The keyboard-shortcuts eval swapped one failure for another (mechanism_table → line_count). This is a net positive: the mechanism table was an abstraction violation, while the line count overshoot is a minor formatting issue from switching to prose.
Due-date-reminders improved the most (+2 assertions) because the baseline had the most egregious over-specification (Prisma syntax, SQL, cron expressions).
Notifications was already clean in baseline (12/12) and remained clean — the skill changes didn't regress completeness.
Time cost increased ~14% — prose-based approaches take slightly longer to generate than terse mechanism mappings. This is acceptable for the quality improvement.
The 150-line limit may need raising to ~160 for complex features where prose-based Technical Approach naturally requires more space than terse implementation prescriptions.
